title: Paphia helenae
taxonomy: Paphia helenae is a species of orchid in the genus Paphia. It is classified as a terrestrial orchid, native to China. Within the Paphia genus, it is often placed in the subgenus Trigonidium. The species Paphia helenae is further classified within the Paphia genus as part of the family Orchidaceae. This classification puts Paphia helenae within the broader category of flowering plants.

intro: Paphia helenae, also known as Paphia, is a type of orchid plant native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Paphia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Paphia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of tinctures and ointments, to leverage its health benefits.
